storyInfo: Titi successfully resolves the dispute at the construction site. But where is Pallas, who should have been the one to do it?

attributes.content: Are you moving or not?!
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: No!
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Are you moving or not?!
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: NO!
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: Wh-What a passionate debate! I can feel the heroic blood in their voices!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: ...But they're not actually saying anything.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: They're just yelling at the top of their lungs. I don't even know what they're saying.
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: The dispute over Athenius's route has been brewing between the fruit farmers and the temple builders for ages.
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: Embarrassing, really, to make a random tourist off the street try to solve our dispute. Heck, even I couldn't tell half of what they're saying...
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: You said you wanted to know what they're saying...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Stop looking for trouble, Titi.
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: This 'random tourist off the street' has an idea.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Hey! Don't just wander in there! Things could get rough!
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: The craftsmen rep and the militiaman arguing on behalf of the fruit farmers are not budging in the slightest, their faces red with anger. They fail to notice the pair of pointy black ears poking out from beneath the stone table.
The tiny Feline girl leaps out and grabs their hands with a grin, bringing them together in a handshake.
Caught off guard, the two reps lose their balance and topple onto the stone table, staring at the jolly intruder.
Hey, who brought this girl to the temple? This is no place for children.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: See, you're so unreasonable, even an outlander is embarrassed. You're a disgrace to Athenius!
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: Stop adding fuel to the fire!
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Listen, girl, this is Minoan business. We don't need some foreigner telling us how to make up.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Who said anything about making up?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Whuh?
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep & Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: The only way to solve a problem is to face it directly. How long do you think the peace would last, even if you made up here and now?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Besides, when in Minos, do as the Minoans do. The heroic epics and fantastical stories of Minos are full of ancient wisdom. You don't need me to tell you how to solve your problems.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: They say the mere mention of Heraces, the greatest Minoan warrior, sent enemies fleeing in terror.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: But he never raised a hand against his fellow Minoans. If someone insulted him, he would challenge them to arm-wrestling.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Settle it with a contest of strength, eh? Sounds good! Spares us a sore throat.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: I'm not done yet.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: There is another legend about a silent hero, who was once a bold and passionate youth, surrounded by flowers and adoration.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: As fate would have it, he learned a forbidden secret, knowledge that no one should possess, so he kept silent for thirty years.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Wait, we know the legend of Heraces, but who's this 'silent hero'? Did you just make him up on the spot?
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Oh, it's a Minoan tale that my father used to tell me. It's rather obscure, but it's a really good parable—
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Ahem. Anyway, I propose that you follow in the footsteps of ancient Minoans, and settle this with an arm-wrestling match.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: But with one extra condition—you only get to talk if you win, no matter how pissed you might be.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Never heard that rule before.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Are you poor with words, or weak of limb?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Enough talk. Let's do it.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Alright! The inaugural First Temple Arm-Wrestling Tournament is on. Ready, set—
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Go!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Well, well, well! The militiaman looks lean, but he's got skill, and he takes round one when the big guy loses focus for a second!
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: You—
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Shut it! Loser doesn't get to talk!
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: New apples can't get out with all the stone piled up at the crossing! The orchards are all in the mountain's shadow, and the apples that haven't ripened yet don't get any sun! The whole harvest's going to the dumps at this rate!
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: One-nil. Round two.
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: Hey, all those muscles ain't for nothing! Looks like hauling marble is good exercise!
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: We can't move the stone until the apples are out of the way! Besides, the building of the First Temple was inspired by the Minoan heroes! Who's gonna take responsibility if the work's delayed? You?
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: One-all. Next!
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Apple harvesting and hauling stone... I see.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Minoans sure are fun to watch! But things will get less interesting when they start getting tired...
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: Hey, why did they stop?
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: They've realized the arm-wrestling isn't about determining a victor, but giving them an opportunity to hear each other out.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: When you're angry, your ears stop working, and even simple things become complicated, much less long-standing disputes.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You're more thoughtful than you look.
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: But how do you solve the core problem, now that you've gotten emotion out of the way?
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: *pant*... *pant*...
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: *pant*... *pant*...
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: Looks like a draw.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Young lady...
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Call me Titi.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: It's not like we screwed the fruit farmers on purpose, Titi.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: We've got a lot of hands in the rebuilding, but they only quarry stone when they please, where they please. I brought fifteen tons of marble down the mountain by myself yesterday, and it's still far from enough.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Look, we understand. But the apples don't care.
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: Increasing quarrying efficiency, while getting enough sun for the apples... why don't we meet in the middle, then?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: Just remove the peak that's blocking the sunlight.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Hahahaha! Some 'middle'! That's the craziest thing I've ever heard!
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: It is intriguing, though.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Out of personal curiosity, I studied the urban planning and natural environment of Athenius on my way here.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The peak that's in the way of the sunlight is only a secondary peak, with soft soil at the top and a large marble layer underneath—it would make an excellent quarry.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You mean we can get sunlight to the orchards by leveling the peak, without having to change the city's route? Is that even possible?
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: The fruit farmers must join the quarriers.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Adding more hands is the quickest way to increase efficiency. We'll also need to get the craftsmen scattered across the mountains together.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: If you can carry fifteen tons of marble a day all by yourself...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Then leveling the peak is not impossible.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: The rebuilding of the temple was the collective decision of the citizens of Athenius. We certainly won't complain, if we can solve the sunlight problem at the same time.
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: I'll go talk to the farmers!
attributes.name: Fruit Farmer Rep

attributes.content: Problem solved!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: All thanks to you, Titi.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: Don't mention it.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: It looks amazing, even in its incomplete state... Now, I'm even more curious about the one running this project.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Speaking of which, we still have a problem.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: What is it?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The original proponent of the First Temple project, Pallas, went missing last night.
attributes.name: Craftsmen Rep

attributes.content: '...Our joy was for both of us, but now eternal peace is yours alone.'
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: 'The breeze in the mountains of Heria and the rivers of the Agnes Plains shall shine in your eyes no more.'
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: 'Neither shall my heart know the tranquility of night and the leisure of day again...'
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It's been seven years, Ithakos, old friend.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: The tall warrior places the cyclamen flowers before the white grave marker.
His movements are light and gentle, but several white petals still fall upon the ground.
You're late.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I'm simply passing through, on my way to a burdenbeast ranch with a view.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You can find all the excuses you want, but you know you can't let it go.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Neither can I.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You haven't once visited the Endymion Temple in your five years in Athenius.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The first and last time you came was when you brought Ithakos's bloody chiton.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I don't need the guidance of the heroes.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: But you continue to dream the same dream. You blame yourself for sending Ithakos across the river, to the battle where he died.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The river runs only in my dreams now, since the Sargonians built a dam upstream.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It brought the melting snow from Heria's peaks to my homeland, and it brought him...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Ithakos was one of the few true friends that I had, one who was never concerned for who I am or what I can do.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: His family was tormented by the fires of war, and his father lived most of his life as a broken man. But he would not accept the same fate. His eyes were bright and eager when he received divine revelation.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Whatever the outcome, he found a friend and a brother in you. Did he ever visit you in your dreams, telling you not to mourn him anymore?
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: It's your place to interpret dreams, and mine to mourn and remember. I will soothe my rage in my own way.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: The war is over. Sargon has signed a non-aggression pact with us, and this visit by the Sargonian mission is a symbol of our new relationship.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I know that. Lydia made a point to put me in my place.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: The first Astynomos in Athenian history elected by popular vote, empowered to handle diplomatic matters and certain public issues independent of the council and the priests...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: She's caused you a fair share of trouble, hasn't she?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Endymion Temple has not been affected yet, but some fringe clerics and the roles of their temples are being marginalized. Besides...
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Kereseira's First Temple is nearing completion. There should be a ceremonial play organized by the priests to call the spirit of Kereseira into the temple and grant Minos her protection.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Now, the play is being organized by the Astynomos' Office.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The Astynomos purported to assent when I discussed this matter with her a few days ago, but she was evasive when it came to the program we'd choose...
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: To her, the priests and their ceremonies are just props to promote Minoan tourism.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Your insistence on using canonized classical drama will only be seen as an act of aggression by the priesthood.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I understand that reform is inevitable, but...
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Some things will not and can not change.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Lydia's little power plays on the administrative side are none of my business, but I won't stand idly by if the Sargonian visitors have ill intent.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I'm no hero, just an ordinary Infected, and I don't need the guidance of divine revelation, but I still dream.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: If I tell you that I dreamt of a river running red with blood, as Ithakos did nine years ago...
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: How would you interpret it?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Xanthos!
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Excuse me. I have work to do at the crossing, and an apprentice waiting for me.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You're really too kind, Titi. Not only did you solve our dispute with the fruit farmers, you're also helping us look for Pallas.
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: It's nothing. Besides, I want to meet the one who came up with the temple project, face-to-face.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: This Pallas was last seen at the crossing?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Yes.
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: She was sitting on the docks, drinking and muttering in the direction of distant Mt. Hymnoi.
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: She's helped us with so many things since the rebuilding of the temple began. Although she doesn't hold any position in the temples of Athenius, and Kassandra from Endymion Temple is the one in charge of the responsibilities that are above-board...
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: Pallas was the one who took care of everything behind the scenes, from the division of labor to procurement to smoothing things over with the right people.
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: I see. It's certainly concerning that someone so reliable would go missing all of a sudden...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Ah, well... it's not the first time.
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: Almost forgot to ask. What does she look like?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: A small Forte lady, always seems like she's half-asleep. She wears a little flower on her head.
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: A little flower... the kind with two leaves, and petals so red you can't see the wrinkles?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Yes! How did you know?
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: Does it look like that?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The craftsman turns in the direction that Titi is pointing, to see a small red flower swaying gently in a pile of apples.
Yes, it does... but that's one of the apple farmers' crates, isn't it? What's a flower doing there?
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: Wait, there's something inside! Duck!
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: It is as though the crate has just woken up, sending ripe apples pouring across the docks, falling into the clear waters of the Delphi Canal.
A Forte slides out from the bed of fruit, rubbing her eyes at the sudden influx of bright sunlight.
P-Pallas?
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: Guys! Guys! No need for the boat! We found Pallas!
attributes.name: Craftsman

attributes.content: This is Pallas, the people's hero, the proponent and reliable supervisor of the rebuilding plan?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: She's not quite what I imagined...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Titi approaches the young woman on the ground curiously.
She examines the horns for signs of battle, trying to determine her weapon's construction, whether it is able to support unique Originium Arts...
She even has the urge to reach for the mysterious little flower, to see if it is a boon from the Minoan heroes.
But Titi senses nothing unusual, except the sweet scent of apples.
The young woman seems to have woken up. Titi leans in to hear what she is muttering.
Mount... Hymnoi...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: The western peak... level it... to let the sunlight...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Tell the farmers and workers... what's missing...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yes... apple cider should be... shared...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Leveling the peak to solve the sunlight problem? So she came to the same conclusion I did... she's thinking about rebuilding the temple, even in her sleep.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Who says a hero has to be big and brawny? How could I forget that I'm in Minos, not Sargon?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Mm... who are you?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: We meet at last, Pallas, first proponent of the rebuilding of Kereseira's First Temple.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I'm Medjehtiqedti Nasutu Mashayer, ambassador from Sargon.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Medj...?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: My friends call me Titi.
attributes.name: Titi
